description_long: This is a modern tiltrotor airplane with a sleek, streamlined fuselage and two large rotors mounted on short, straight wings extending from either side. The rotors are positioned at the wingtips and are capable of vertical rotation, allowing for both vertical takeoff and forward flight. The aircraft features a forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with a pointed nose and multiple windows, providing clear visibility for pilots. The tail section is equipped with a T-tail configuration, with a horizontal stabilizer mounted atop a single vertical fin. The landing gear appears to be a tricycle type, with visible main gear under the wings and a nose gear beneath the cockpit. The exterior is primarily white with dark accents, and the aircraft bears registration markings and insignia on the fuselage and tail.
description: A modern tiltrotor airplane with large wingtip rotors, a T-tail, and a forward enclosed cockpit, finished in white with dark accents.
description_view_0: Side view showing the tiltrotor configuration, T-tail, and registration markings on the fuselage.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the twin rotors, narrow fuselage, and forward cockpit windows.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the tiltrotor setup, T-tail, and additional insignia details.
description_view_3: Rear view emphasizing the T-tail, twin rotors, and the overall width of the wingspan.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Mid-wing configuration with straight wings and large tiltrotor nacelles at the wingtips; no visible winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Twin turboprop engines mounted at the wingtips within tiltrotor nacelles.
attribute_tail_design: T-tail configuration with a single vertical stabilizer and a high-mounted horizontal stabilizer.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Tricycle landing gear with two main gear units under the wings and a single nose gear.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with multiple windows, likely multi-seat.
